examples.title: Typename Keyword Usage Example
examples.description: Demonstrates correct use of typename when accessing nested types
examples.explanation: The typename keyword is required when accessing T::value_type and T::pointer because these are dependent names (they depend on template parameter T). Without typename, the compiler cannot determine if these are types or static members.

examples.title: Template Template Parameter Example
examples.description: Shows template template parameters with both class and typename
examples.explanation: Template template parameters can use either 'class' or 'typename'. In C++17 and later, 'typename' is preferred for consistency. This example shows both forms for template template parameters.

examples.title: Dependent Name Resolution
examples.description: Complete example of dependent name resolution with typename
examples.explanation: This example demonstrates multiple contexts where typename must be used: variable declarations, function return types, and anywhere a dependent nested type is referenced. The std::enable_if and SFINAE pattern also uses typename extensively.
